originalClaim: Knockdown of STAT1 abolished the interferon-gamma response signature, with strong downregulation of interferon-stimulated genes including IRF1, OAS1, and MX1.
evidenceSummary: Differential expression in the STAT1 knockdown arm shows a coordinated collapse of the canonical ISG core across all IFN-γ-stimulated replicates, consistent with STAT1's role as the central transcription factor downstream of IFNGR signaling.
supportedParts: STAT1 knockdown significantly reduces IFN-γ response pathway activity (GSEA NES -3.1, FDR < 1e-6).
IRF1, OAS1, and MX1 are each significantly downregulated (log2FC < -1.5, adj. p < 0.001) in STAT1-KD vs non-targeting control.
missingEvidence: No direct measurement of STAT1 protein-level knockdown efficiency (mRNA knockdown only).
saferRewrite: Knockdown of STAT1 significantly reduced interferon-gamma response pathway activity, with IRF1, OAS1, and MX1 among the most strongly downregulated interferon-stimulated genes.

evidenceSources.title: Perturb-seq Differential Expression: STAT1 KD vs NTC (IFN-γ, 24h)
evidenceSources.excerpt: IRF1 log2FC -2.14 (adj p=3.2e-9); OAS1 log2FC -1.87 (adj p=1.1e-7); MX1 log2FC -1.62 (adj p=4.4e-6). 412 of 431 core ISGs significantly downregulated.
evidenceSources.citation: DE table, n=3 replicates, MAST test

evidenceSources.title: GSEA: Hallmark Interferon Gamma Response
evidenceSources.excerpt: NES = -3.08, FDR q < 1e-6 for STAT1-KD vs NTC contrast, 187/200 leading-edge genes downregulated.

originalClaim: TP53 perturbation induced a robust apoptotic program across all cell types in the screen.
evidenceSummary: The apoptotic signature following TP53 perturbation is real but restricted to a subset of proliferative cell states; two of five annotated cell types show no significant enrichment.
supportedParts: TP53 knockout cells show significant upregulation of the apoptosis hallmark gene set in proliferating epithelial-like clusters (NES +2.4, FDR < 0.01).
CDKN1A and BAX are both upregulated in the same clusters (adj p < 0.01).
unsupportedParts: "Across all cell types" is not supported: myeloid and quiescent clusters show no significant apoptosis enrichment (FDR > 0.3).
Effect size in the two non-significant clusters is close to zero, inconsistent with a uniform, cell-type-independent program.
missingEvidence: No cleaved-caspase or annexin-V functional validation — transcriptional proxy signature only.
saferRewrite: TP53 perturbation induced a significant apoptotic transcriptional signature in proliferative cell clusters, but this effect was not detected in myeloid or quiescent populations.

evidenceSources.title: GSEA: Hallmark Apoptosis, by Cell-Type Cluster
evidenceSources.excerpt: Epithelial-like cluster: NES +2.41, FDR q=0.004. Fibroblast-like cluster: NES +1.98, FDR q=0.02. Myeloid cluster: NES +0.31, FDR q=0.58. Quiescent cluster: NES +0.12, FDR q=0.81.

evidenceSources.title: Perturb-seq Differential Expression: TP53 KO vs NTC
evidenceSources.excerpt: CDKN1A log2FC +1.72 (adj p=2.1e-8); BAX log2FC +0.94 (adj p=0.003), effects concentrated in proliferating clusters.
evidenceSources.citation: DE table, n=4 replicates, MAST test

originalClaim: PDCD1 (PD-1) knockout increased effector cytokine production in CD8+ T cells, consistent with checkpoint blockade phenotypes.

conditions: PDCD1 CRISPR KO, CD8+ T cells, anti-CD3/CD28 restimulation

evidenceSummary: PDCD1 KO increases IFNG transcription on restimulation, matching the expected checkpoint-release phenotype, but TNF and degranulation markers are unchanged, so "effector cytokine production" as a general claim overreaches.
supportedParts: IFNG is significantly upregulated in PDCD1-KO CD8+ T cells after restimulation (log2FC +1.1, adj p=0.004).
Direction of effect matches the expected checkpoint-blockade phenotype.
unsupportedParts: TNF shows no significant change (log2FC +0.08, adj p=0.71).
GZMB/PRF1 degranulation markers are not significantly altered, so the general claim of increased "effector cytokine production" overstates a single-gene effect.
missingEvidence: No protein-level cytokine secretion data (ELISA/CyTOF) in this dataset — transcriptional readout only.
saferRewrite: PDCD1 knockout increased IFNG transcription in restimulated CD8+ T cells, consistent with a partial checkpoint-blockade phenotype, though TNF and degranulation markers were unaffected.

evidenceSources.title: Perturb-seq Differential Expression: PDCD1 KO vs NTC, CD8+ T cells (restimulated)
evidenceSources.excerpt: IFNG log2FC +1.14 (adj p=0.004); TNF log2FC +0.08 (adj p=0.71); GZMB log2FC +0.11 (adj p=0.62); PRF1 log2FC -0.04 (adj p=0.89).
evidenceSources.citation: DE table, n=3 replicates, MAST test

originalClaim: FOXP3 loss completely reprogrammed regulatory T cells into a Th17-like state.
evidenceSummary: FOXP3 loss destabilizes the canonical Treg transcriptional program, but there is no coordinated induction of the Th17 program — RORC and IL17A are not upregulated — so the claimed lineage conversion is not supported by this dataset.
supportedParts: FOXP3 knockout significantly reduces canonical Treg identity genes (IL2RA, CTLA4, IKZF2; adj p < 0.01).
unsupportedParts: RORC shows no significant change (adj p=0.52).
IL17A is not detected above background in either condition.
No shift toward a Th17-like transcriptional cluster is observed in cluster-proportion analysis.
missingEvidence: No lineage-tracing or fate-mapping data to assess stability of any reprogramming over time.
No direct Th17 functional assay (IL-17 secretion).
saferRewrite: FOXP3 loss destabilized the regulatory T cell identity program (reduced IL2RA, CTLA4, IKZF2), but did not induce a Th17-like transcriptional signature in this dataset.

evidenceSources.title: Perturb-seq Differential Expression: FOXP3 KO vs NTC, Treg-sorted
evidenceSources.excerpt: IL2RA log2FC -1.41 (adj p=1.8e-6); CTLA4 log2FC -1.05 (adj p=3.4e-5); RORC log2FC +0.09 (adj p=0.52); IL17A: below detection threshold in both arms.
evidenceSources.citation: DE table, n=3 replicates, MAST test

evidenceSources.title: Cluster Proportion Analysis: FOXP3 KO vs NTC
evidenceSources.excerpt: No significant shift into Th17-annotated clusters (chi-sq p=0.44). Majority of FOXP3-KO cells occupy an intermediate/de-differentiated Treg cluster, not a Th17 cluster.

originalClaim: NLRP3 inflammasome activation was the primary driver of the observed pyroptosis signature in myeloid cells.
evidenceSummary: The myeloid-restricted pyroptosis signature co-occurs with NLRP3, CASP1, and GSDMD pathway activation, and is substantially blunted in the NLRP3 knockout arm, supporting a primary causal role.
supportedParts: Pyroptosis gene signature score is significantly elevated in myeloid clusters upon NLRP3 agonist treatment (p < 0.001).
NLRP3 knockout reduces the pyroptosis signature by 74% relative to wild-type under the same stimulus.
missingEvidence: No direct GSDMD cleavage (Western blot) validation — signature-based inference only.
saferRewrite: NLRP3 inflammasome activation was a major driver of the pyroptosis signature in myeloid cells, supported by strong attenuation of the signature upon NLRP3 knockout.

evidenceSources.title: Pyroptosis Signature Score by Perturbation, Myeloid Subset
evidenceSources.excerpt: NLRP3 agonist vs untreated: signature score +2.9 SD (p<0.001). NLRP3-KO + agonist vs WT + agonist: 74% reduction in signature score (p<0.001).

originalClaim: MYC overexpression uniformly increased proliferation across every perturbed cell population in the dataset.
evidenceSummary: MYC overexpression increases proliferation scores in most, but not all, perturbed populations; one cluster shows a paradoxical decrease consistent with oncogene-induced senescence.
supportedParts: 6 of 7 perturbed cell populations show significantly increased proliferation/cell-cycle scores under MYC overexpression (adj p < 0.05).
unsupportedParts: "Uniformly" and "every" are contradicted by cluster 4, which shows a significant decrease in proliferation score (adj p=0.002), consistent with oncogene-induced senescence rather than growth.
missingEvidence: No competitive growth or dropout assay to confirm the proliferation phenotype at the functional level.
saferRewrite: MYC overexpression increased proliferation scores in most perturbed cell populations, with one notable exception showing a signature consistent with oncogene-induced senescence rather than increased growth.

evidenceSources.title: Proliferation Signature Score by Cluster, MYC-OE vs NTC
evidenceSources.excerpt: Clusters 1-3, 5-7: proliferation score +0.4 to +1.6 SD (adj p<0.05). Cluster 4: proliferation score -0.7 SD (adj p=0.002); senescence signature score +1.1 SD (adj p=0.01).

originalClaim: IL2RA perturbation had no measurable effect on T cell exhaustion markers.

conditions: IL2RA perturbation — not present in current screen panel

evidenceSummary: IL2RA is not among the perturbations included in this Perturb-seq screen, and exhaustion marker readouts (TOX, HAVCR2, LAG3) were not collected in the associated experiment, so this claim cannot be checked against available evidence.
missingEvidence: IL2RA is not a targeted guide in the available perturbation panel.
No exhaustion marker panel (TOX, HAVCR2, LAG3, PDCD1) was profiled in any matched condition.
saferRewrite: The effect of IL2RA perturbation on T cell exhaustion markers could not be evaluated — IL2RA is not included in the current perturbation panel, and exhaustion markers were not profiled in a matched condition.

evidenceSources.title: Perturb-seq Screen v3 — Guide Panel & Coverage Manifest
evidenceSources.excerpt: 142 targeting guides across 71 genes. IL2RA: not targeted. Exhaustion marker panel (TOX, HAVCR2, LAG3): not included in this experiment's readout set.

originalClaim: Combined knockout of BAX and BAK1 rescued cells from apoptosis induced by NLRP3 activation, though a residual cell death signature persisted, suggesting a minor BAX/BAK1-independent pathway.
evidenceSummary: BAX/BAK1 double knockout substantially reduces the apoptosis signature under NLRP3 activation, and a smaller residual death signature is indeed present in the double-knockout condition, matching both parts of this claim.
supportedParts: Apoptosis signature score is reduced by 81% in BAX/BAK1-DKO vs wild-type under NLRP3 agonist (adj p<0.001).
A statistically significant residual cell-death-associated signature (19% of wild-type magnitude) remains in the DKO condition (adj p=0.01), consistent with the claimed residual pathway.
unsupportedParts: The claim that the residual pathway is "BAX/BAK1-independent" is a mechanistic interpretation not directly tested — the residual signature could also reflect incomplete knockout efficiency rather than a distinct pathway.
missingEvidence: No knockout efficiency / protein validation to rule out incomplete BAX/BAK1 depletion as the source of residual signal.
No test of an alternative death pathway (e.g., pyroptosis via GSDMD) to confirm a mechanistically distinct route.
saferRewrite: Combined knockout of BAX and BAK1 substantially reduced NLRP3-induced apoptosis, with a smaller but statistically significant residual cell-death signature persisting — consistent with, but not proof of, a minor BAX/BAK1-independent pathway.

evidenceSources.title: Apoptosis Signature Score: BAX/BAK1-DKO vs WT, + NLRP3 Agonist
evidenceSources.excerpt: WT + agonist: apoptosis score +3.2 SD. DKO + agonist: apoptosis score +0.6 SD (81% reduction, adj p<0.001 vs WT; residual signal adj p=0.01 vs unstimulated DKO).
